channel.title: The New Beverly Cinema
channel.description: Schedule and program for The New Beverly Cinema (Quentin Tarantino's revival theater in Los Angeles).

item.title: Crimson Tide / Déjà Vu
item.description:
Dynamic Denzel Washington ( The Pelican Brief ) joins Hollywood favorite Gene Hackman ( The Firm ) in this intense action thriller that’s a smash hit with audiences and critics alike! In the midst of a global crisis, the USS Alabama receives an unconfirmed order to launch its nuclear missiles – signaling the start of World War III! The tension quickly rises as the sub’s respected commander (Hackman) and his brilliant executive officer (Washington) clash over the validity of their orders… battling each other for control of the sub! As this epic struggle rages under the sea, Crimson Tide brings motion picture excitement to a new level – and you to the edge of your seat! (Hollywood Pictures) Academy Award winner Denzel Washington (Best Actor, Training Day , 2001) joins forces with blockbuster producer Jerry Bruckheimer and mega-hit director Tony Scott for Déjà Vu – the powerful, fast-paced action-thriller with a spectacular mind-bending twist. Called in to recover evidence in the aftermath of a horrific explosion on a New Orleans ferry, Federal agent Doug Carlin (Washington) gets pulled away from the scene and taken to a top-secret government lab that uses a time-shifting surveillance device to help prevent crime. But can it help Carlin change the past? (Disney)
Cast: Director: Tony Scott | Writer: Screenplay by Michael Schiffer, Story by Michael Schiffer and Richard P. Henrick | Starring: Denzel Washington, Gene Hackman, Matt Craven, George Dzundza, Viggo Mortensen, James Gandolfini | Year: 1995 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Tony Scott | Writer: Bill Marsilii & Terry Rossio | Starring: Denzel Washington, Paula Patton, Bruce Greenwood, Adam Goldberg, Jim Caviezel | Year: 2006 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 126 minuti

Orari: Sat May 30 - 5:30 pm / 7:55 pm

item.title: The Black Arrow / Fortunes of Captain Blood
item.description:
In this crackling adaptation of the Robert Louis Stevenson tale, a knight returns from the War of the Roses only to learn that his father was murdered under mysterious circumstances. When he sets out to discover the truth, he finds his own life in jeopardy. A cast full of familiar favorites drives this lively adventure, including Louis Hayward, Janet Blair, George Macready, Edgar Buchanan and Rhys Williams, and directed by Gordon Douglas. (Columbia Pictures) Louis Hayward portrays Captain Peter Blood, a former doctor who takes to piracy after he is exiled from England for treating the wounds of an enemy soldier. During his travels, Blood meets the beautiful Isabelita (Patricia Medina), who happens to be the niece of Blood’s arch-nemesis, the Marquis de Riconete (George Macready). (Sony Pictures)
Cast: Director: Gordon Douglas | Writer: Screen play by Richard Schayer & David P. Sheppard & Thomas Seller | Starring: Louis Hayward, Janet Blair, George Macready, Edgar Buchanan | Year: 1948 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Gordon Douglas | Writer: Screenplay by Michael Hogan and Robert Libott and Frank Burt based upon the novel by Rafael Sabatini | Starring: Louis Hayward, Patricia Medina, George Macready, Alfonso Bedoya, Dona Drake, Lowell Gilmore | Year: 1950 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 90 minuti

Orari: Wed May 27 - 7:30 pm / 9:15 pm

item.title: Crimson Tide / Déjà Vu
item.description:
Dynamic Denzel Washington ( The Pelican Brief ) joins Hollywood favorite Gene Hackman ( The Firm ) in this intense action thriller that’s a smash hit with audiences and critics alike! In the midst of a global crisis, the USS Alabama receives an unconfirmed order to launch its nuclear missiles – signaling the start of World War III! The tension quickly rises as the sub’s respected commander (Hackman) and his brilliant executive officer (Washington) clash over the validity of their orders… battling each other for control of the sub! As this epic struggle rages under the sea, Crimson Tide brings motion picture excitement to a new level – and you to the edge of your seat! (Hollywood Pictures) Academy Award winner Denzel Washington (Best Actor, Training Day , 2001) joins forces with blockbuster producer Jerry Bruckheimer and mega-hit director Tony Scott for Déjà Vu – the powerful, fast-paced action-thriller with a spectacular mind-bending twist. Called in to recover evidence in the aftermath of a horrific explosion on a New Orleans ferry, Federal agent Doug Carlin (Washington) gets pulled away from the scene and taken to a top-secret government lab that uses a time-shifting surveillance device to help prevent crime. But can it help Carlin change the past? (Disney)
Cast: Director: Tony Scott | Writer: Screenplay by Michael Schiffer, Story by Michael Schiffer and Richard P. Henrick | Starring: Denzel Washington, Gene Hackman, Matt Craven, George Dzundza, Viggo Mortensen, James Gandolfini | Year: 1995 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Tony Scott | Writer: Bill Marsilii & Terry Rossio | Starring: Denzel Washington, Paula Patton, Bruce Greenwood, Adam Goldberg, Jim Caviezel | Year: 2006 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 126 minuti

Orari: Sun May 31 - 6:30 pm / 8:55 pm

item.title: The Black Arrow / Fortunes of Captain Blood
item.description:
In this crackling adaptation of the Robert Louis Stevenson tale, a knight returns from the War of the Roses only to learn that his father was murdered under mysterious circumstances. When he sets out to discover the truth, he finds his own life in jeopardy. A cast full of familiar favorites drives this lively adventure, including Louis Hayward, Janet Blair, George Macready, Edgar Buchanan and Rhys Williams, and directed by Gordon Douglas. (Columbia Pictures) Louis Hayward portrays Captain Peter Blood, a former doctor who takes to piracy after he is exiled from England for treating the wounds of an enemy soldier. During his travels, Blood meets the beautiful Isabelita (Patricia Medina), who happens to be the niece of Blood’s arch-nemesis, the Marquis de Riconete (George Macready). (Sony Pictures)
Cast: Director: Gordon Douglas | Writer: Screen play by Richard Schayer & David P. Sheppard & Thomas Seller | Starring: Louis Hayward, Janet Blair, George Macready, Edgar Buchanan | Year: 1948 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Gordon Douglas | Writer: Screenplay by Michael Hogan and Robert Libott and Frank Burt based upon the novel by Rafael Sabatini | Starring: Louis Hayward, Patricia Medina, George Macready, Alfonso Bedoya, Dona Drake, Lowell Gilmore | Year: 1950 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 90 minuti

Orari: Thu May 28 - 7:30 pm / 9:15 pm

item.title: Crimson Tide / Déjà Vu
item.description:
Dynamic Denzel Washington ( The Pelican Brief ) joins Hollywood favorite Gene Hackman ( The Firm ) in this intense action thriller that’s a smash hit with audiences and critics alike! In the midst of a global crisis, the USS Alabama receives an unconfirmed order to launch its nuclear missiles – signaling the start of World War III! The tension quickly rises as the sub’s respected commander (Hackman) and his brilliant executive officer (Washington) clash over the validity of their orders… battling each other for control of the sub! As this epic struggle rages under the sea, Crimson Tide brings motion picture excitement to a new level – and you to the edge of your seat! (Hollywood Pictures) Academy Award winner Denzel Washington (Best Actor, Training Day , 2001) joins forces with blockbuster producer Jerry Bruckheimer and mega-hit director Tony Scott for Déjà Vu – the powerful, fast-paced action-thriller with a spectacular mind-bending twist. Called in to recover evidence in the aftermath of a horrific explosion on a New Orleans ferry, Federal agent Doug Carlin (Washington) gets pulled away from the scene and taken to a top-secret government lab that uses a time-shifting surveillance device to help prevent crime. But can it help Carlin change the past? (Disney)
Cast: Director: Tony Scott | Writer: Screenplay by Michael Schiffer, Story by Michael Schiffer and Richard P. Henrick | Starring: Denzel Washington, Gene Hackman, Matt Craven, George Dzundza, Viggo Mortensen, James Gandolfini | Year: 1995 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Tony Scott | Writer: Bill Marsilii & Terry Rossio | Starring: Denzel Washington, Paula Patton, Bruce Greenwood, Adam Goldberg, Jim Caviezel | Year: 2006 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 126 minuti

Orari: Fri May 29 - 5:30 pm / 7:55 pm

item.title: Django Unchained
item.description:
Quentin Tarantino’s hit western stars Jamie Foxx as Django Unchained , a slave freed by bounty hunter Dr. King Schultz (Christoph Waltz) who needs his help tracking down a trio of wanted outlaws. In exchange, Schultz agrees to help Django free his wife from evil plantation owner Calvin Candie (Leonardo DiCaprio). To a bounty hunter it’s just business but to Django it’s personal! “In Django , Tarantino is a man unchained , creating his most articulate, intriguing, provoking, appalling, hilarious, exhilarating, scathing and downright entertaining film yet.” – Betsy Sharkey, Los Angeles Times
Cast: Director: Quentin Tarantino | Writer: Quentin Tarantino | Starring: Jamie Foxx, Christoph Waltz, Leonardo DiCaprio, Kerry Washington, Samuel L. Jackson, Walton Goggins, Dennis Christopher, James Remar | Year: 2012 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 165 minuti

Orari: Fri May 29 - 11:59 pm

item.title: Show People / The Matinee Idol
item.description:
Marion Davies has stars in her eyes in this “delightful look at silent Hollywood” (Time Out Film Guide), directed by King Vidor and costarring William Haines. All Peggy Pepper (Davies) wants in life is to be a great dramatic actress. But when rowdy slapstick comic Billy Boyle (Haines) gives Peggy her big break, she puts her dreams on hold and becomes America’s comedy queen. So when prestigious High Arts Studio offers her the chance to play serious roles and Peggy gets an awfully swelled head, it’s up to Billy to burst her bubble and bring Peggy down to earth. Loosely based on Gloria Swanson’s career. Show People features cameos by Charlie Chaplin, Douglas Fairbanks, John Gilbert, William S. Hart and more in this “bright and snappy ode to Hollywood stardom” (Allrovi.com). (Warner Bros) A rarely screened early silent film from director Frank Capra! When Ginger Bolivar’s amateur stock company is invited to join superstar Don Wilson’s Broadway review, little does Ginger (Bessie Love) know that recently hired bit-part actor Harry Mann (Johnnie Walker) and Don are one and the same. Don, you see, believes the oh-so-serious Bolivar Players are oh-so-unintentionally funny, and has brought them to the Big Apple as a comedy act. But when “Harry” falls for Ginger, Don soon learns that humiliating the woman he loves is no laughing matter! (Sony)
Cast: Director: King Vidor | Starring: Marion Davies, William Haines, Dell Henderson, Paul Ralli | Year: 1928 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Frank Capra | Starring: Bessie Love, Johnny Walker, Ernest Hilliard, Lionel Belmore, David Mir | Year: 1928 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 56 minuti

Orari: Sun May 24 - 6:30 pm / 8:25 pm

item.title: The Victors
item.description:
The award-winning screenwriter of The Bridge on the River Kwai and The Guns of Navarone , Carl Foreman, wrote, directed, and produced the sprawling, all-star World War II epic The Victors . The film follows a large ensemble of battle-weary American infantry soldiers in a series of vignettes, from fighting in Italy to uneasy peace in Berlin, interspersed with newsreel-type propaganda from back home that contradicts their grim reality. The movie’s monumental shoot filmed on location across Europe with an impressive cast that includes Vince Edwards, Albert Finney, George Hamilton, and George Peppard, as well as Jeanne Moreau, Romy Schneider, and Elke Sommer.
Cast: Director: Carl Foreman | Starring: Vince Edwards, Albert Finney, George Hamilton, Melina Mercouri, Jeanne Moreau, George Peppard, Maurice Ronet, Rosanna Schiaffino, Romy Schneider, Elke Sommer, Eli Wallach | Year: 1963 | Country: UK/USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 155 minuti

Orari: Tue May 26 - 7:30 pm

item.title: The Victors
item.description:
The award-winning screenwriter of The Bridge on the River Kwai and The Guns of Navarone , Carl Foreman, wrote, directed, and produced the sprawling, all-star World War II epic The Victors . The film follows a large ensemble of battle-weary American infantry soldiers in a series of vignettes, from fighting in Italy to uneasy peace in Berlin, interspersed with newsreel-type propaganda from back home that contradicts their grim reality. The movie’s monumental shoot filmed on location across Europe with an impressive cast that includes Vince Edwards, Albert Finney, George Hamilton, and George Peppard, as well as Jeanne Moreau, Romy Schneider, and Elke Sommer.
Cast: Director: Carl Foreman | Starring: Vince Edwards, Albert Finney, George Hamilton, Melina Mercouri, Jeanne Moreau, George Peppard, Maurice Ronet, Rosanna Schiaffino, Romy Schneider, Elke Sommer, Eli Wallach | Year: 1963 | Country: UK/USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 155 minuti

Orari: Mon May 25 - 7:30 pm

item.title: Diamonds Are Forever (IB Tech Print)
item.description:
Sean Connery is back as Bond, reuniting with his Goldfinger director Guy Hamilton for a thrilling adventure speeding from the canals of Amsterdam to the neon-soaked streets of Las Vegas. Our debonair 007 must infiltrate a dangerous diamond smuggling ring where he uncovers an even more diabolical plot from his most nefarious nemesis.
Cast: Director: Guy Hamilton | Writer: Screenplay by Richard Maibaum and Tom Mankiewicz | Starring: Sean Connery, Jill St. John, Charles Gray, Lana Wood, Jimmy Dean, Bruce Cabot, Putter Smith, Bruce Glover, Norman Burton, Joseph Furst, Bernard Lee, Desmond Llewelyn | Year: 1971 | Country: UK | Format: I.B. Technicolor 35mm
Durata: 120 minuti

Orari: Sun May 24 - 2:00 pm

item.title: Cinderella (IB Tech Print)
item.description:
Walt Disney’s timeless animated masterpiece delightfully dazzles in sparkling I.B. Technicolor 35mm! When her cruel stepmother and mean-spirited siblings block the quiet but kindhearted Cinderella from attending the Royal Ball, she’ll find help from her Fairy Godmother and a few mice friends. But with their spell breaking at the stroke of midnight, she’ll lose a single glass slipper that will fatefully become the key to her fairytale ending.
Cast: Director: Hamilton Luske, Wilfred Jackson, Clyde Geronimi | Year: 1950 | Country: USA | Format: I.B. Technicolor 35mm
Durata: 74 minuti

Orari: Sat May 30 - 2:00 pm

item.title: Princess Mononoke (Subtitled)
item.description:
The fate of the world rests on the courage of one warrior in director Hayao Miyazaki’s breathtaking Studio Ghibli epic. A young warrior searching for the cure to a deadly curse finds himself in the middle of a bitter struggle between the leader of Iron Town and the mysterious Princess Mononoke , a young girl raised by wolves, who will ferociously fight to protect her forest home. English subtitled version
Cast: Director: Hayao Miyazaki | Writer: Hayao Miyazaki | Year: 1997 | Country: Japan | Format: English subtitled 35mm
Durata: 134 minuti

Orari: Sat May 30 - 11:59 pm

item.title: Cinderella (IB Tech Print)
item.description:
Walt Disney’s timeless animated masterpiece delightfully dazzles in sparkling I.B. Technicolor 35mm! When her cruel stepmother and mean-spirited siblings block the quiet but kindhearted Cinderella from attending the Royal Ball, she’ll find help from her Fairy Godmother and a few mice friends. But with their spell breaking at the stroke of midnight, she’ll lose a single glass slipper that will fatefully become the key to her fairytale ending.
Cast: Director: Hamilton Luske, Wilfred Jackson, Clyde Geronimi | Year: 1950 | Country: USA | Format: I.B. Technicolor 35mm
Durata: 74 minuti

Orari: Sun May 31 - 2:00 pm
